english: pf. reṅs (rengs) to be stiff, hard, rigid, rmai reṅ sbyaṅs (rma'i reng sbyangs) to remove the hard parts, of a wound (to clear, to cleanse) Wdn.; *raṅs soṅ* (*rangs song*) W. (the blood) has coagulated, congealed, also of a dead body: it has grown stiff; *ráṅs-te ḍad dug* (*rangs te ḍad dug*) W. he makes himself stiff, he struggles against; réṅs-pa (rengs pa) solid (opp. to liquid), coagulated, stiff, hard; reṅs-par byed-pa (rengs par byed pa) to make hard or stiff; fig.: stiffnecked, obstinate, unwilling, Do.
1. pastil for fumigating Lt., v. spos (spos). — 2. Sch.: separate, not belonging to anything else.
sometimes for raṅs (rangs), v. nyin-reṅs (nyin rengs), ṭo-reṅs (tho rengs).

1. to be, = yin-pa (yin pa), in Sp. and C., rarely in B.; also ré-pa (re pa) (ré-ba (re ba)) is met with; k'yed p'yugs-rdzi ma red rdo-rje-sems-dpar snaṅ (khyed phyugs rdzi ma red rdo rje sems dpar snang) you are not a herdsman, no, you are Vajrasattva (viz. a deity)! Pth.; *ćaṅ yó'-pa re' mé'-pa re'*? (*cang yod pa re/ med pa re/*) is there any beer here or not? C. — 2. Cs.: to be ready, red mda (red mda') a ready arrow Cs.; red daṅ ma red rma (red dang ma red rma) a healed wound and one not yet healed (?) Sch. — 3. to be withered Ts.
Sch.: to be in a great haste or hurry, to be very zealous, W.: *reb log ćò-ćé* (*reb log co ce*) to do something wicked again and again.

vb. and adj. (to be) strong, vigorous, durable, sound, hearty, of men and animals, *rem-pa soṅ* (*rem pa song*) W. now I feel strong again; *gyóg-pa ḍúl-će-la rém-pa ćo!* (*gyog pa ḍul ce la rem pa co!*) W. exert yourself to walk fast! čos spyod rem (chos spyod rem) show your ability, in performing ceremonies or incantations Mil.; rém-čig rém-čig ḍré-tsogs-rnams (rem cig rem cig 'dre tshogs rnams) be strong, ye hobgoblins, show your power; do your best! (ironically) Mil.
Mil. prob. panel or square, of a wainscoted wall, of a chessboard etc.; re(u)-mig (re'u mig) id.
termin. of re (re), to each individually; . . . a piece.

1. inst. of re (re). — 2. change, turn, time, times, da ṅed byéd-pai rés-la báb-ste (da nged byed pa'i res la bab ste) it being now our turn of acting Dzl.; *dí-riṅ ču-ré koṅ yod* (*di ring chu re kong yod*) W. to-day it is his turn to irrigate (the field); res byéd-pa (res byed pa) with verbal root, to do a thing by turns with another person, čáṅ-la ,tuṅ-rés byéd-pa (chang la 'thung res byed pa), resp.: skéms-la gsol-rés mdzád-pa (skems la gsol res mdzad pa) to vie with one another in drinking beer Glr.; skyes ,bul-rés byéd-pa (skyes 'bul res byed pa) to send mutual presents to one another Glr.; res 'jóg-pa (res 'jog pa) to change Sch.; rés-kyis (res kyis) relieving one another (in service), doing (a thing) alternately or by turns, e.g. nyál-la mél-tse byéd-pa (nyal la mel tse byed pa) to sleep and to keep watch Dzl.; res (res) is also used as an adv.: 1. res če res čuṅ (res che res chung) now great, now small, or partly great, partly small; res yod res med (res yod res med) at one time it is there, at another not Cs. 2. at a time, every time, distributively: res p'ye t'ur-mgo re tsam ,gams (res phye thur mgo re tsam 'gams) I always take the tip of a spoon full of meal at a time Mil.; res gčig (res gcig) once, once upon a time Tar., res ,ga (res 'ga') sometimes, res . . . res (res . . . res) now — now, at one time — at another, frq.; *lu-ré* (*lu re*) W. a change of singing, an alternative song; rés-mos (res mos) v. re-mos (re mos); res-gzá (res gza') a changing (wandering) star, a planet Cs.; res-,grogs-zla-skár (res 'grogs zla skar) the stars with which the moon is successively in conjunction Sch.

sbst. taste, flavour, savour, k'a-ro (kha ro) id.; ro-myóṅ-ba (ro myong ba) to taste; six different kinds of taste are distinguished: mṅár-ba (mngar ba) sweet, skyúr-ba (skyur ba) sour, lán-tswa-ba (lan tswa ba) salt, k'á-ba (kha ba) bitter, tsá-ba (tsha ba) acrid, bská-ba (bska ba) astringent, and the medicines accordingly are also divided into six classes; ro brgya daṅ ldan-pa (ro brgya dang ldan pa) of a hundred-fold taste, i.e. of the most exquisite and manifold flavour, frq. — II. sbst. 1. also ró-ma (ro ma)? resp.: spur (spur), dead body, corpse, carcase, mi-ro (mi ro) a dead man, rta-ro (rta ro) dead horse, srin-bui ro (srin bu'i ro) dead insects Dzl.; ro srég-pa (ro sreg pa) to burn a corpse. — 2. body, v. comp. — 3. residue, remains, sediment, tság-ro (tshag ro) or ,tság-ro ('tshag ro) that which remains in a sieve or filter, impurities, husks etc., já-ro (ja ro) tea-leaves in a tea-pot, tsíl-ro (tshil ro) the remains of bacon after having <
